passed: Empty day presents a clear invitation
Trimmed non-empty priorities add by Enter and pointer
Three-slot cap is enforced and visibly explained; completion does not create a fourth slot
Priorities toggle complete and incomplete and can be removed independently
Progress is visible as a count, three-segment visual, and named ARIA progressbar
All three completed priorities produce an unmistakable complete state
Reload preserves current-day priorities and completion states under daymark:v1
Reset cancellation preserves data and reset confirmation clears it
Stale-day and malformed records are cleared safely with no user-facing history
TypeScript/Vite production build succeeds and dist/ runs locally
390x844 and 1280x900 Chromium flows pass without horizontal overflow
Semantic accessible names and a visible 3 px keyboard focus outline were verified
Browser run recorded only localhost GETs, with no external request, network write, console error, page error, failed request, or HTTP error
Scoped static security, secrets, dependency, and data-flow review found no actionable finding
README contains exact install, run, test, build, and production-preview commands
Possible pack, skill lock, outcome brief, workstream handoffs, fresh review, and this receipt are present
resolvedHistoricalFailures: Product draft initially disagreed with the confirmed storage schema and reset presentation; corrected before integration and rechecked
First proof run failed because of the test environment's incomplete localStorage; deterministic test-only storage fixed it
Second proof run failed because of proof matcher assumptions; proof-only assertions were corrected
First browser run sampled a declared color transition too early; the settled rerun passed
One ingredient-audit shell loop used zsh's read-only status variable; corrected audit passed
One combined secret-scan shell pattern was quoted incorrectly; corrected scans passed
skipped: A real wall-clock midnight wait; rollover was tested deterministically instead
Firefox, WebKit, physical-device, and screen-reader runs
Online advisory refresh, remote DAST, penetration testing, fuzzing, and security or accessibility certification
Real-user validation, customer-demand testing, and production reliability or scale testing
Hosting-layer headers and deployment smoke checks because deployment was outside scope

knownLimitations: Browser evidence is from headless Chromium only, not Firefox, WebKit, a physical device, or a screen reader
Rollover depends on the browser clock and timezone; a real midnight was not awaited
The offline advisory audit may not contain advisories published after the local cache was last refreshed
The scoped review is not a security guarantee, penetration test, accessibility certification, or production-readiness claim
Priority text is same-origin local-storage data and is not encrypted; local storage is not a confidentiality boundary against future same-origin code or a compromised browser profile
No user research or real-user validation was performed
